reference: Hello. I can understand your concern regarding your symptoms. one) Never attempt to manually remove stool. Instead, it is advisable to use a stool softener or laxatives. two) The habit of manually removing stool and constipation can develop an anal fissure, which is a small tear on the inner wall of the lower part of the anal canal. Constipation and manual removal of stool may require a proctoscopy and rectal examination. For an anal fissure, you may consider using a stool softener or laxative, analgesics for anal pain, and anesthetic medication ointment for local application.
